summary: Matched the remaining lbcommand functions by replacing inline assembly for Command_04 and Command_09 with C and by refining CommandInfo offset 0x08 into a union with command-specific interpretations. The PR body explicitly notes that Command_N_Struct structs were created for use in a union at offset 0x8 because different commands treat the same storage differently, which was needed for matching.
what_changed: Changed CommandInfo::data_position at offset 0x08 into a union named u containing data_position, Command_04, and Command_09 interpretations.
Added Command_04_Struct with a u32* ptr[1] member for Command_04-specific access patterns.
Added Command_09_Struct with bitfields id:6, param_1:8, and param_2:18 to model the packed command word consumed by Command_09.
Updated existing command handlers Command_00 through Command_08 to access the data pointer through info->u.data_position.

Replaced inline assembly implementation of Command_04 with C that decrements loop state through a raw u32* view of CommandInfo, conditionally jumps data_position through the Command_04 union view, or advances data_position and subtracts two from loop_count.
Replaced inline assembly implementation of Command_09 with C using Command_09_Struct and calling func_80021C48(cmd->param_1, cmd->param_2), then advancing data_position.
Removed local pragma push/pop and peephole directives around Command_Execute after the surrounding functions were matched in C.
smart_moves: Recognized that offset 0x08 of CommandInfo is not semantically just a u32* for every command and modeled it as a union rather than forcing one incorrect field type.
Used command-specific structs for the same storage location, enabling clearer C expressions while preserving layout-sensitive matching.
Modeled Command_09's packed command word as bitfields, allowing the C implementation to express the original rlwinm/clrlwi extraction as named parameters.
For Command_04, used a u32* cast of CommandInfo to reproduce indexed access into overlapping loop/event state while still keeping most logic expressed through CommandInfo fields.
decomp_lessons: When several command handlers reinterpret the same offset differently, a union on the owning struct can be the matching-friendly representation.
Command interpreter structs may need per-opcode typed views rather than a single globally meaningful field name.
Packed command data can sometimes be matched more cleanly with bitfield structs if the bit widths line up with the observed masks and shifts.
Array sizes in partially understood structs may be provisional; the existing event_return[4] comment notes the array size was made up for now, so future work should validate it against more users.
Replacing inline asm may require accepting temporary low-level C patterns, such as raw word indexing into a struct, when the real layout or aliasing is still not fully understood.
naming_conventions: Command handlers use the existing Command_NN naming pattern, such as Command_00, Command_04, and Command_09.
New per-command layout helpers use Command_N_Struct names, specifically Command_04_Struct and Command_09_Struct.
The union inside CommandInfo is named u, with members data_position, Command_04, and Command_09.
Bitfield members in Command_09_Struct are named id, param_1, and param_2.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Converted Command_04 from inline PowerPC assembly to C while preserving the offset-based behavior of accesses around loop_count and event_return.
Converted Command_09 from inline PowerPC assembly to C by representing the command word as Command_09_Struct bitfields and calling func_80021C48 with extracted parameters.
Used a union at offset 0x08 to satisfy different code-generation needs for data_position, Command_04 pointer indexing, and Command_09 packed parameter access.
Removed peephole pragma scaffolding around Command_Execute, suggesting the surrounding code no longer needed that local compiler control after matching.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that rename or validate Command_04_Struct, Command_09_Struct, or the CommandInfo union members.
Search for additional users of CommandInfo::event_return to confirm the provisional array size and loop-state layout.
Search for later decomp work around func_80021C48 to determine the semantic names of Command_09 param_1 and param_2.
Check whether later cleanup replaces the raw u32* cast in Command_04 with a better-understood struct layout.
